name: Chained Together
short_description: From the depths of hell, climb chained to your friends through diverse worlds. Solo or co-op, try to reach the summit and discover what awaits you there...
detailed_description:
Dive into the epic adventure of "Chained Together"

Begin your journey in the depths of hell, chained to your companions. Your mission is to escape hell by climbing as high as possible.
Each jump requires perfect coordination to scale the platforms and escape the scorching heat. Traverse a multitude of worlds, each offering unique challenges.

Diverse Worlds to Explore

Each world introduces new challenges.

Solo or Multiplayer Mode

Whether you're going it alone or teaming up with friends locally, the game adapts to fit the number of players, supporting local multiplayer for up to 4 players.

Realistic Chain Physics

The chain is not just a visual element. It is governed by a physics simulation that allows it to wrap around platforms.

3 Game Modes

The game features several difficulty modes: Beginner, Normal, and Lava mode.

  • Beginner: An option in the pause menu allows you to teleport back to the highest point you reached before a significant fall.
  • Normal: No specific help, you fall in case of failure and must start over.
  • Lava: You must climb quickly before the lava engulfs you.
The leaderboard and Steam achievements are only valid in Normal and Lava modes.

Leaderboards

Track your escape speeds with leaderboards that compare completion times across different player configurations.



Whether solo or in cooperation with up to 4 players in multiplayer mode, each step brings you closer to the summit!

reviews.review_text:
“Family bonding has never gone this off the rails.”

I grabbed Chained Together thinking it’d be a chill co-op game to mess around with my parents.
Yeah… no.
This game is basically a friendship (and family) stress test disguised as a platformer.

Not even 10 minutes in:
My mom is screaming like something’s actually chasing her
My dad is calling out moves like we’re in a war zone
And somehow I’m getting blamed for every single fall… even when I’m the one holding it together

It turned into a nonstop loop of:
“DON’T MOVE.”
“WHY ARE YOU MOVING?!”
“I SAID DON’T—”
and we’re all falling again
At one point we all agreed to “take it slow.”
That lasted maybe 7 seconds before my dad launched us off the edge like we were all tied to a cursed bungee cord.

Best moments:
Hearing my parents say things I KNOW they’ve never said before
Realizing none of us have coordination, but we all think we do
Watching teamwork fall apart in real time

Worst parts:
You start keeping track of who messed up like it’s a scoreboard
“One more run” turns into 20
You will absolutely question your life choices

But honestly…
I haven’t laughed that hard in a long time.
Total chaos, nonstop yelling, zero teamwork and somehow still fun.

Would I recommend it? Yeah, for sure.
Would I run it back with my parents? …I need a little time after that one.
nice if youre not playing with more than 2 ppl lmao

me and my girlfriend got together because of this game, we played this and we became friends instantly. recommend 100%
